persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypothesis: with 11 Goode officials and 4 Goode biographies in the corpus, the stint could belong to a different William Goode, e.g. a relative who also reached North Borneo. This fails: the stint is the unique office of Governor and Commander-in-Chief of North Borneo, held by one person at a time, and the biography names exactly this office with exactly these years: 'gov. and c.-in-c., N. Borneo, 1960-63' (stint listed 1960-1963). The honours sequence corroborates independently: the bio records K.C.M.G. (1958) and G.C.M.G. (1963), and the staff list shows the same officer as K.C.M.G. in 1960-62 then G.C.M.G. in 1963 — a year-accurate honour transition that a namesake would not reproduce. Forename 'William' agrees in full. No second knighted William Goode could have held the same governorship over the same span.
persons.would_overturn: External evidence that a different William Goode held the North Borneo governorship 1960-63, or that the bio's final clause was conflated from another entry.

notes: The position_sim FAIL is an abbreviation artifact ('gov. and c.-in-c.' = 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief'). Exact position+place+year-range agreement, full forename, and a year-accurate K.C.M.G.-to-G.C.M.G. honour transition constitute multiple independent dimensions. Bio timeline row 17 ('dist. offr., Raub, 1963') is an OCR misreading of 1936 in version col1953-L17469.v, corrected in other versions; it does not affect this link.
